properties.remark: CHP REPORTED WATER AND MUD ACROSS INTERSTATE 80 JUST EAST OF SODA SPRINGS. CALTRANS TO PUT UP SLOW/FLOODING SIGNS. NO SLOWING NOTED ON KINGVALE/TRUCKEE CAMS.
SAND SPRINGS NV DOT SITE
TREE WHICH WAS 2 FEET IN DIA AND ABOUT 20 FEET TALL FELL ON FENCE. NUMEROUS SMALL BRANCHES FROM OTHER TREES ALSO BROKEN.
FALLON AGRIMET SITE
WIND EST 50 MPH WITH BROKEN WRIST SIZE BRANCHES DOWN IN YARD.
CARSON SINK DRI SITE. GUSTING ABOVE 50 MPH SINCE 540 PM PDT
HEAVY RAIN REPORTED WITH QUARTER SIZE HAIL.
SPOTTER REPORTED QUARTER SIZED HAIL AND VERY HEAVY RAINFALL. SPOTTER ALSO REPORTED LOCALIZED FLOODING ON PROPERTY, BUT NO MAJOR CONCERNS.
STRONG WINDS ESTIMATED AT 40 TO 50 MPH WITH BLOWING DUST RESTRICTING VISIBILITY TO LESS THAN 1/4 MILE...DOWN TO 100 FEET AT TIMES. LOCATION NEAR WILSON CANYON AND HIGHWAY 208 IN LYON COUNTY.
REPORT PASSED FROM CHURCHILL COUNTY SHERRIFFS OFFICE. MIDDLEGATE STATION REPORTED BAD FLASH FLOODING EAST OF MIDDLEGATE ON NV HIGHWAY 722 AND U.S. HIGHWAY 50 BETWEEN EASTGATE AND MIDDLEGATE.
RISING WATER IN DRY CREEK BEDS TO NEAR BANKFULL ALONG HIGHWAY 50. WATER STILL RISING FROM STORM 1 HOUR AGO 5 MILES SOUTHEAST.
SPOTTER REPORTED 3/4 INCH TO 1 INCH HAIL.
ESMERALDA COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED LARGE AMOUNT OF WATER FLOWING OVER INTERSECTION OF HWY 168 AND 266.
AT GALENA RAWS EL. 5610 FEET. OUTFLOW FROM CONVECTION OVER LAKE TAHOE.
AT TOULON NDOT SITE.

A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER IN SILVER SPRINGS REPORTED A DUST STORM WITH VISIBILITY LESS THAN 1 MILE. AREAS AFFECTED INCLUDED LAHONTAN RESERVOIR AND HIGHWAYS 50 AND 95A. WIND GUSTS WERE ESTIMATED AT 40 MPH.
PUBLIC REPORT OF 1 INCH HAIL ON THE NEVADA SIDE AT BORDERTOWN.
PUBLIC REPORT OF 1 INCH HAIL ON THE NEVADA SIDE AT BORDERTOWN.

THE NEVADA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED THAT THE WESTBOUND LANE OF INTERSTATE 80 WAS CLOSED ABOUT 20 MILES EAST OF FERNLEY DUE TO POOR VISIBILITY CAUSED BY BLOWING DUST. TWO TRACTOR-TRAILER RIGS RAN OFF THE ROAD WHEN THE VISIBILITY WAS NEAR ZERO.
NEAR ZERO VISIBILITY IN BLOWING DUST.
SPOTTER REPORTED NEAR ZERO VISIBILITIES ON PYRAMID HWY NEAR LAZY 5 PARK.
SPOTTER REPORTED VERY LIMITED VISIBILITY AND WIDESPREAD BLOWING DUST.
SPOTTER REPORTED 1/4 MILE VISIBILITY.
AT MOUNT ROSE SUMMIT ON NV HIGHWAY 431. EL. 8889 FT.
SUSTAINED WINDS ESTIMATED BETWEEN 40 AND 50 MPH ARE CAUSING BLOWING DUST AND VISIBILITY LESS THAN 1 MILE AT TIMES.

*** 1 FATAL, 2 INJ *** NEVADA HIGHWAY PATROL IS REPORTING A LARGE ACCIDENT ON INTERSTATE 80 BETWEEN FERNLEY AND LOVELOCK WITH 1 FATALITY AND 2 PERSONS TRAPPED. THE ACCIDENT INVOLVED 4 TRACTOR-TRAILERS AND 2 PICKUP TRUCKS. NHP REPORTED VISIBILITIES OF ZERO DUE TO BLOWING DUST.
